title: Driver booking dispatch system (assigned bookings inbox + actions)

requirements.text: Add backend APIs to support a driver dispatch workflow: (1) allow an authenticated driver to fetch a list of bookings assigned to them, sorted by newest first, and (2) allow an assigned driver to update the status of their own booking to one of: accepted, refused, completed, or cancelled (and when cancelled/refused, store an optional reason). Enforce authorization so only the assigned driver (or admin where appropriate) can access these APIs.

quotes: Add booking dispatch system to driver

acceptanceCriteria: Calling the new 'get my assigned bookings' query as a driver returns only bookings where assigned_driver == caller, sorted by booking id descending (matching existing admin sorting behavior).
Calling the new 'driver update booking status' method as the assigned driver updates that booking only; non-assigned users are rejected with a clear trap message.
Admin-only methods (e.g., getAllBookings, updateBookingStatus, assignDriver) remain admin-only and unchanged in behavior.
If a driver provides a refusal/cancellation reason, it is persisted on the booking (e.g., via cancel_reason) and returned in subsequent reads.

requirements.text: Create a driver dispatch UI that lets an authenticated driver view and manage their assigned bookings: include an “Assigned Bookings” list with key details (booking id, pickup/destination, pickup time, customer name/phone, current status), a detail panel, and clear actions to Accept, Refuse (with required reason), Mark Completed, and Cancel (with required reason).

quotes: Add booking dispatch system to driver

acceptanceCriteria: A driver can navigate to a dedicated dispatch screen and see only their assigned bookings.
Selecting a booking shows full details and available actions appropriate to its current status.
Refuse/Cancel actions require a non-empty reason before submission and show inline validation errors if missing.
After any status mutation, the UI refreshes using React Query invalidation so the list and details reflect the updated status.

requirements.text: Add navigation/routes for the driver dispatch workflow: introduce a route for the dispatch inbox (e.g., /driver/dispatch) and ensure drivers can easily jump from an assigned booking to the existing location update page (/driver/track/$bookingId). Add a prominent entry point from the Profile page (driver profile) to the dispatch inbox.

quotes: Add booking dispatch system to driver

acceptanceCriteria: A new route exists for a driver dispatch inbox and is registered in the TanStack Router route tree.
From the dispatch booking details, the driver can navigate to the existing location update screen for that booking id.
The Profile page shows an “Open Dispatch” (or similar) button/section only when the caller has a driver profile.

requirements.text: Add React Query hooks for driver dispatch: provide a query hook for fetching the caller’s assigned bookings and mutation hooks for driver status updates (and reason when applicable). Ensure query keys and invalidation patterns are consistent with existing booking/tracking hooks.

quotes: Add booking dispatch system to driver

acceptanceCriteria: Frontend has dedicated hooks for: (1) get assigned bookings for caller, (2) driver update booking status with optional reason.
On mutation success, relevant queries are invalidated (assigned bookings list, and any booking detail/tracking queries for the affected booking).
Hooks gracefully handle missing actor/identity similar to existing hooks (no crashes).

requirements.text: Apply a cohesive driver-dispatch visual theme consistent with the app’s existing warm/amber styling: clear hierarchy for “new/active/completed” states, high-contrast status badges, and mobile-first layout that works well in the Android build.

quotes: Add booking dispatch system to driver

acceptanceCriteria: Dispatch UI is readable and usable on narrow mobile screens without horizontal scrolling.
Statuses are visually distinct (e.g., Pending/Accepted/Completed/Cancelled/Refused) using consistent badge styles.
Primary actions (Accept/Refuse/Complete/Cancel/Update Location) are visually prominent and not easily mis-tapped.

constraints: Backend must remain a single Motoko actor (all logic in backend/main.mo).
Do not edit files under frontend/src/components/ui or any other immutablePaths listed in SYSTEM_CONTEXT; compose existing UI components instead.
Use English for all user-facing text.
nonGoals: Implementing real-time dispatch via WebSockets/push notifications.
Integrating external maps/GPS/geolocation services for automatic driver location capture.
Third-party authentication providers beyond Internet Identity.
